extraction.one_line_summary: GREIPEL exploited GROENEWEGEN's premature 300m jump and DÉMARE's positioning error to win in crosswinds that briefly split ALAPHILIPPE from the field
tactical_patterns: ALAPHILIPPE caught in late crosswind split despite yellow jersey, suggesting ITT exhaustion created vulnerability on stage following maximum efforts
Quick-Step deployed full team chase to close ALAPHILIPPE's crosswind gap rather than relying on peloton neutralization, revealing jersey defense concern
10km headwind destroyed organized lead-outs enabling GREIPEL's positional experience advantage over younger sprinters in chaotic finish
GROENEWEGEN's 300m attack timing error shows WorldTour road champion lacks sprint distance judgment compared to veterans in headwinds

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: vulnerable to late crosswind splits day after maximal ITT, needed full team extraction despite yellow jersey
rider_signals.GREIPEL André: positive: won chaotic headwind sprint through superior positioning when lead-outs collapsed, capitalized on GROENEWEGEN's timing error
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: premature 300m jump in headwind showed inexperience despite national champion status, allowed GREIPEL to follow and pass
rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: negative: positioning too far back in final kilometer to contest despite having speed, tactical placement failure cost stage
rider_signals.GALLOPIN Tony: neutral: teammate GREIPEL's win benefits morale while holding second overall at 33 seconds
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: neutral: holding KOM since stage one but must infiltrate breakaway on 48-point stage six to retain jersey
